perturbation.original_text: (b) "Contract Territory" shall mean the states of Indianapolis and Tennessee and Company's e-commerce site.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) "Contract Territory" shall mean the states of Indianapolis and Tennessee, Company's e-commerce site, and any location where Endorsed Products are sold through authorized retailers.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the definition of 'Contract Territory'. This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 2(b) obligates Company to prevent the sale of Endorsed Products outside the original, narrower Contract Territory. The modified definition now makes it unclear where the Company is responsible for preventing sales, thus contradicting with section 2(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Company agrees that during the Contract Period it will use its best efforts actively and aggressively to (i) promote the sale of all Endorsed Products in the Contract Territory, and (ii) prevent the sale of any Endorsed Products outside the Contract Territory. Failure of Company to comply with the provisions of this Section 2(b) shall entitle Pey Dirt to revoke this license immediately (notwithstanding any other provision in this Agreement to the contrary).
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Contract Territory' to include any location where Endorsed Products are sold through authorized retailers. This directly contradicts the original obligation in Section 2(b), which requires the Company to prevent the sale of Endorsed Products *outside* the (now expanded) Contract Territory. The change creates an ambiguity, as it's unclear where the Company is responsible for preventing sales, since the territory is broadened. Therefore, the definition of a term changed, causing in-text ambiguities, as laid out in the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Remuneration. (a) Within thirty (30) days following the conclusion of each Contract Year Quarter, Company shall deliver to Pey Dirt an itemized statement setting forth the total shipments of Endorsed Products during said Contract Year Quarter and, at the same time, shall pay to Pey Dirt a royalty with respect to such shipments as hereinafter provided.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Remuneration. (a) Within sixty (60) days following the conclusion of each Contract Year Quarter, Company shall deliver to Pey Dirt an itemized statement setting forth the total shipments of Endorsed Products during said Contract Year Quarter and, at Company's convenience during the quarter, shall pay to Pey Dirt a royalty with respect to such shipments as hereinafter provided.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the payment terms. Originally, payment was due within thirty days. Now, the altered text pushes this timeframe to 60 days for the statement and introduces uncertainty around the timing of payment. Section 12(a)(i) allows termination for failure to make payment within ten days of written notice. This contradicts with section 4(a) modified text, if the company fails to pay within 30 days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) If either party at any time during the Contract Period shall (i) fail to make any payment of any sum of money herein specified to be made, or (ii) fail to observe or perform any of the covenants, agreements or obligations hereunder (other than the payment of money), the non-defaulting party may terminate this Agreement as follows: as to subparagraph (i) if such payment is not made within ten (10) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice of such failure to make payment, or as to subparagraph (ii) if such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice specifying in reasonable detail the nature of such default. In order to be a sufficient notice hereunder, any such written notice shall specify in detail each item of default and shall specify the provision of this Agreement which applies to each item of default, and shall specify in detail the action the defaulting party must take in order to cure each such item of default. The termination rights set forth in this section shall not constitute the exclusive remedy of the nondefaulting party hereunder, however, and if default is made by either party hereunder, the other may resort to such other remedies as said party would have been entitled to if this section had been omitted from this Agreement. Termination under the provisions of this section shall be without prejudice to any rights or claims which the terminating party may otherwise have against the defaulting party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 4(a) changes the payment terms, introducing uncertainty and extending the timeframe for both statement delivery and payment. This directly contradicts Section 12(a)(i), which allows termination if payment isn't made within ten days of written notice, effectively creating a conflict on how long company has to make payment. This creates a contradiction because section 4(a) suggests that payment can be made at the company's convenience during the quarter (90 days), and the original text said 30 days following each Contract Year Quarter. This means that they can technically be late in paying, which triggers the clause 12(a)(i). This means that the company now has 90 days plus an additional ten days (after written notice) to make a payment, creating the inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Pey Dirt and Manning, or either of them, from and against any and -2- all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, arising out of, or in any way connected with, any advertising material furnished by, or on behalf of, Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Pey Dirt and Manning, or either of them, from and against any and -2- all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, arising out of, or in any way connected with, any advertising material furnished by, or on behalf of, Company, provided such material was explicitly approved by Pey Dirt in writing, and such approval shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.explanation: This modification adds a condition to Company's indemnity obligation. The original text unconditionally requires Company to indemnify Pey Dirt and Manning for advertising-related expenses, etc. Now, Company's obligation is conditional on Pey Dirt providing explicit written approval and not unreasonably withholding it. This intext contradiction arises if read with Section 3. Approvals, specifically the line 'Company agrees that no use whatsoever of Manning Identification nor any item used in connection with Manning Identification (including, without limitation, advertising) will be made hereunder unless and until the same has been approved by Pey Dirt', since the changed text now means that there are now conflicting obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company agrees that no use whatsoever of Manning Identification nor any item used in connection with Manning Identification (including, without limitation, advertising) will be made hereunder unless and until the same has been approved by Pey Dirt. Pey Dirt agrees that any material, advertising or otherwise, submitted for approval as provided herein may be deemed by Company to have been approved hereunder if the same is not disapproved in writing within fourteen (14) days after receipt thereof. Pey Dirt agrees that any material submitted hereunder will not be unreasonably disapproved and, if it is disapproved, that Company will be advised of the specific grounds therefor. Company agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Pey Dirt and Manning, or either of them, from and against any and -2- all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, arising out of, or in any way connected with, any advertising material furnished by, or on behalf of, Company, provided such material was explicitly approved by Pey Dirt in writing, and such approval shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a condition (explicit written approval by Pey Dirt) to Company's indemnity obligation, which directly conflicts with the prior condition in the 'Contradicted Text', which stipulates that Company's use of Manning Identification in advertising is contingent on Pey Dirt's approval, further clarifying that approval can be assumed if no written disapproval is received within 14 days. This discrepancy creates ambiguity regarding the conditions under which Company is obligated to indemnify Pey Dirt, and a in-text contradiction, since there are two potential approval processes. This meets the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition, as the key obligation regarding advertising approval is defined inconsistently. It also introduces a structural flaw and misaligned terminology, as there is a conditional obligation of whether or not Pey Dirt's express approval is needed in writing.
